jokes.text: B-E-N-D-E-R Bender B-E-N-D-E-R, Bender Saying B-E-N-D!
jokes.setup: Bender singing his own name like a jingle or song
jokes.punchline: The abrupt ending on 'B-E-N-D!' instead of completing 'BENDER'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bender's narcissistic personality. The truncated ending adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Mail call! Amy, your designer lingerie catalog. Fry, Sadie's Bra Parade. And Leela, Bulk Underpants Outlet.
jokes.setup: Delivery of mail catalogs to characters
jokes.punchline: The progression from designer to bulk underpants, ending on practical Leela
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Amy's vanity, Fry's perversion, and Leela's practicality through catalog choices.

jokes.text: You buy once, you're on the list forever.
jokes.setup: The awkward mail delivery
jokes.punchline: Universal truth about mailing lists
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation that resonates with audience experience.

jokes.text: I hope it was one of my enemies. Those guys suck.
jokes.setup: Learning someone he knows has died
jokes.punchline: Hoping it's an enemy with casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - selfish, callous, but in a funny way. The casual 'those guys suck' sells it.

jokes.text: Well, I'm rich. Goodbye, losers, whom I've always hated!
jokes.setup: Uncle died and left him something in the will
jokes.punchline: Immediately assumes he's rich and abandons his friends with formal insult
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - greedy, disloyal, but the formal language 'whom I've always hated' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Pardon me, my good simpleton. Could you take us to yon castle?
jokes.setup: Asking for directions
jokes.punchline: Using archaic formal language while calling the person a simpleton
jokes.explanation: Bender's mix of politeness and insult, with archaic language adding flavor.

jokes.text: Some say unholy things happen up there. For example, all of us say that.
jokes.setup: Ominous warning about the castle
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that 'some say' means literally everyone
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of the cliche 'some say' with precise literal interpretation. Great structure.

jokes.text: Mumbo, perhaps. Jumbo, perhaps not.
jokes.setup: Bender dismissing warnings as 'mumbo jumbo'
jokes.punchline: Taking the phrase literally and being uncertain about the 'jumbo' part
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that deconstructs 'mumbo jumbo' in an unexpected way. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Yes, you idiot! The circuit diagram is here on the inside of your case.
jokes.setup: Villager asking if science can explain robot consciousness
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact response that it's literally printed inside
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor - dismissive and scientific, deflating mysticism with cold facts.

jokes.text: I choose to believe what I was programmed to believe.
jokes.setup: Being shown scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about belief and programming
jokes.explanation: Smart commentary on faith vs. science, with robot twist making it meta about artificial consciousness.

jokes.text: Filings to filings. Rust to rust.
jokes.setup: Funeral service for robot uncle
jokes.punchline: Robot version of 'ashes to ashes, dust to dust'
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay adapting funeral liturgy for robots. Clean and economical.

jokes.text: I leave a pittance to be paid in 20 installments of 1/20th of a pittance each.
jokes.setup: Reading will bequests
jokes.punchline: Overly complex way to describe giving someone nothing
jokes.explanation: Funny mathematical absurdity - making nothing sound official and complex.

jokes.text: Is that a lot?
jokes.setup: Just inherited $10 million
jokes.punchline: Clueless question about whether it's a significant amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect characterization of spoiled rich kid who has no concept of money.

jokes.text: Oh, man! There's always a catch.
jokes.setup: Learning he has to spend one night in the castle to inherit it
jokes.punchline: Bender's deflated reaction to the condition
jokes.explanation: Predictable response but fits Bender's lazy, scheming character.

jokes.text: I get a good vibe from this place. Nice long dinner table, quiet, well-behaved spiders, graveyard-adjacent.
jokes.setup: Bender surveying his new creepy castle
jokes.punchline: Finding positive aspects in obviously sinister features
jokes.explanation: Great escalating list where each 'positive' gets more ominous. 'Well-behaved spiders' is inspired.

jokes.text: So does my butt, but I don't frame it and put it on the wall. Although...
jokes.setup: Professor's explanation about motion-detecting eyes
jokes.punchline: Bender comparing to his butt, then considering actually displaying it
jokes.explanation: Pure Bender - crude comparison followed by genuine consideration of the absurd idea.

jokes.text: Don't be silly, man. The last ghost died 200 years ago.
jokes.setup: Fry saying the place is haunted
jokes.punchline: Logical impossibility - ghosts can't die
jokes.explanation: Nice logical paradox delivered matter-of-factly. The absurdity is in treating it as reasonable.

jokes.text: They said that about being alive!
jokes.setup: Ghost uncle saying 'you'll like being dead'
jokes.punchline: Bender's pessimistic response about life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - dark, pessimistic, but delivered with comic timing. Very quotable line.

jokes.text: Of course! It was so obvious! That sequence of words I said made perfect sense.
jokes.setup: Professor giving technobabble explanation for ghost hologram
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment that his explanation was nonsensical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where the show acknowledges its own technobabble through the Professor's self-congratulation.

jokes.text: It crept along on round rubber feet, like a wolf!
jokes.setup: Describing the vehicle that ran him over
jokes.punchline: Comparing car wheels to wolf feet
jokes.explanation: Nicely absurd description mixing mechanical and animal imagery.

jokes.text: You've been drinking too much. Or too little. Anyway, not the right amount.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to see things, suggesting drinking problem
jokes.punchline: Covering all possible drinking amounts as wrong
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - sets up binary choice then eliminates all possibilities. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I just need sleep. I had nightmares about cars running people over. Many dream of a fresh new look. And Mary Kay can make those dreams come true.
jokes.setup: Bender talking about violent nightmares
jokes.punchline: Amy treating it as beauty consultation opportunity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Amy's obliviousness to serious situation, focusing only on makeover.

jokes.text: Robot porno theater? I was in that general area last night.
jokes.setup: News report about hit-and-run at robot porno theater
jokes.punchline: Bender casually admitting he was there
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - no shame about being at inappropriate venue, makes him more suspicious.

jokes.text: Yeah? Could a big hallucinating baby do this?
jokes.setup: Being called a hallucinating baby
jokes.punchline: Bender presumably doing something destructive to prove his point
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy setup. The humor is in Bender's indignant response.

jokes.text: I don't know which I like more, smashing cars or faces.
jokes.setup: Elzar at demolition derby
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of enjoying violence against people
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from celebrity chef character. Treats violence casually.

jokes.text: It was probably just a golden marmoset.
jokes.setup: Hearing suspicious sounds
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and unlikely explanation
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment - weirdly specific animal that makes no sense in context.

jokes.text: Don't sound like no golden marmoset I ever heard.
jokes.setup: Amy's marmoset explanation
jokes.punchline: Hermes implying he has extensive marmoset experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback that builds on the absurdity by treating marmoset expertise as normal.

jokes.text: Please, I'm scared. We all are. It's the human condition. That's why I put on this tough guy facade. Now beat it!
jokes.setup: Bender asking for help because he's scared
jokes.punchline: Robot explaining his tough act then immediately using it
jokes.explanation: Nice psychological insight followed by immediate demonstration of the defense mechanism.

jokes.text: Relax. You were probably just shooting big fuzzy craps.
jokes.setup: Bender worried about the dice in his chest
jokes.punchline: Fry's innocent misunderstanding of what the dice might be for
jokes.explanation: Fry's naivety creates innocent interpretation of sinister evidence.

jokes.text: You have nothing to worry about. Except a nightmarish life of unremitting horror! For you see, you are a were-car.
jokes.setup: Reassuring opening statement
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with terrifying diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure - false reassurance immediately undercut by horror. Great dramatic irony.

jokes.text: That's crazy. Yes. So crazy that it must be true!
jokes.setup: Bender calling the diagnosis crazy
jokes.punchline: Psychiatrist using that as proof it's correct
jokes.explanation: Standard 'crazy enough to be true' trope but delivered with good timing.

jokes.text: I can't keep running people over. I'm not famous enough to get away with it. Help me!
jokes.setup: Bender worried about consequences of were-car curse
jokes.punchline: Implying celebrities can get away with vehicular manslaughter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that satirizes celebrity privilege while staying true to Bender's self-centered worldview.

jokes.text: Sure you can die! You want to die? No! There's still too many things I don't own!
jokes.setup: Discussion about mortality and the curse
jokes.punchline: Bender's materialistic reason for wanting to live
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - reduces existential question to pure materialism. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Each night, your attacks will become more gruesome until one dark night you are doomed to kill your dearest friend.
jokes.setup: Explaining the escalating curse
jokes.punchline: The ultimate consequence being killing his best friend
jokes.explanation: Sets up the werewolf trope for the robot context. Solid but predictable horror parody.

jokes.text: Could one of you just--? Ah, forget it. I'll just sleep here.
jokes.setup: Bender chained up to prevent were-car transformation
jokes.punchline: Giving up on asking for help and accepting uncomfortable situation
jokes.explanation: Bender's resignation to discomfort. The visual of him chained up adds to the humor.

jokes.text: What about welding everyone except me to the wall?
jokes.setup: Looking for solutions to prevent him from hurting others
jokes.punchline: Suggesting imprisoning everyone else instead of himself
jokes.explanation: Perfectly selfish Bender logic - solve the problem by inconveniencing everyone else instead of himself.

jokes.text: You jerk! I thought I was your best friend! What kind of two-timing kill-mobile are you?
jokes.setup: Bender tried to kill Leela instead of Fry
jokes.punchline: Fry being jealous about not being the murder target
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - being jealous about not being selected for murder. Great character moment for loyal but naive Fry.

jokes.text: Could you help me? Bender crushed my foot. Stop rubbing it in!
jokes.setup: Leela asking for help with her injury
jokes.punchline: Fry treating medical need as bragging about being attacked
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Fry's jealousy - interpreting Leela's pain as showing off.

jokes.text: Words. Nothing but sweet words turning into bitter orange wax in my ears.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to reassure him about friendship
jokes.punchline: Poetic but bizarre metaphor for not believing him
jokes.explanation: Beautiful absurd poetry from Fry. The specific 'bitter orange wax' detail makes it memorable.

jokes.text: I wish I was stupid and violent. We'd see what's what. I'd pound him till--
jokes.setup: Fry expressing frustration with the situation
jokes.punchline: Wishing for traits he arguably already has
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Fry is often portrayed as not particularly bright and occasionally violent.

jokes.text: We aren't ignorant villagers. We're sophisticated New New Yorkers. Whup his butt!
jokes.setup: Claiming to be sophisticated urbanites
jokes.punchline: Immediately calling for violence in unsophisticated manner
jokes.explanation: Good satire of mob mentality. The immediate contradiction between sophisticated and 'whup his butt' works well.

jokes.text: The natives called it the abominable snowmobile.
jokes.setup: Story about being attacked by motorized sled
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'abominable snowman'
jokes.explanation: Clean pun that creates absurd monster. Short and effective.

jokes.text: I once had a best friend who liked to smell things with me.
jokes.setup: Talking about killing his best friend
jokes.punchline: Describing friendship in terms of shared smelling activities
jokes.explanation: Oddly touching and absurd description of friendship. The innocence makes the tragedy funnier.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa! You're 1000 years old? Your bio says you're 27.
jokes.setup: Calculon claiming to be ancient
jokes.punchline: Fry catching him in age-related lie like a Hollywood actor
jokes.explanation: Good satire of actors lying about their age. Fry's focus on the bio detail is funny.

jokes.text: Acting Unit 0.8, Thespo-mat, David Duchovny.
jokes.setup: Listing his past acting identities
jokes.punchline: Including real actor David Duchovny as if he's a robot
jokes.explanation: Funny escalation ending with real actor. Implies Duchovny might be a robot.

jokes.text: Knight Rider wasn't evil. His windshield wipers were. It didn't come up in the show.
jokes.setup: Fry objecting that Knight Rider wasn't evil
jokes.punchline: Calculon insisting the windshield wipers specifically were evil
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd specificity. The idea that windshield wipers have secret evil lives is inspired.

jokes.text: Look, everyone loves killing people, but I don't wanna hurt my friends.
jokes.setup: Project Satan expressing love for murder
jokes.punchline: Bender casually agreeing that everyone loves killing but drawing line at friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark Bender logic - treating murder as universal hobby but having friendship standards. Extremely quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, no! There's no exhaust pipe! That's right, thanks to Ed Begley Jr.'s electric motor, the most evil propulsion system ever conceived!
jokes.setup: Trying to kill Project Satan by plugging exhaust pipe
jokes.punchline: Electric car being called most evil because of environmental actor
jokes.explanation: Funny inversion - eco-friendly technology as ultimate evil. Ed Begley Jr. reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: Oh, boy! I feel like a car in a candy store!
jokes.setup: Multiple weapons/tools available to kill Project Satan
jokes.punchline: Reversing 'kid in candy store' to match his car form
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay adapting common phrase to were-car situation. Clean and economical.

jokes.text: Yes! He chose me! He's trying to kill me! Leela, I'm so happy!
jokes.setup: Bender attacking Fry instead of Leela
jokes.punchline: Fry's joy at being the murder target
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Fry's jealousy arc. His genuine happiness at being attacked is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: I must have killed him! He's my best friend and I killed him! I never felt so empty inside.
jokes.setup: Thinking he killed Fry
jokes.punchline: Bender's uncharacteristic genuine emotion and regret
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of genuine emotion from Bender. Works because it's so out of character.

jokes.text: That's my last beer, you bastard! I'll kill you! I'll kill you too, buddy. I'll kill you too.
jokes.setup: Fry drinking Bender's beer after emotional moment
jokes.punchline: Immediate return to normal murder threats, but now affectionate
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending - back to normal Bender behavior but with 'buddy' showing their bond has strengthened.
